info.title: Totalis RFQ API
info.description:
Public REST surface for the Totalis parlay RFQ platform — a decentralized request-for-quote marketplace for parlay bets across Kalshi and Polymarket, with on-chain Solana vault settlement.

**Wire format**: snake_case JSON. All successful responses are wrapped in `{ "data": ... }`; list endpoints add `meta` with cursor-based pagination. Errors use a `{ "error": { code, message, details? } }` envelope.

**Authentication**: programmatic clients send `X-API-Key`; the web dashboard uses Privy JWTs (`Authorization: Bearer ...`). Any authenticated user can both place parlays and quote as a market maker — there is no separate MM role. Admin endpoints are out of scope for this public reference.

**Rate limiting**: 100 req/min anonymous, 300 req/min authenticated. Responses include `X-RateLimit-*` headers. Request body limit: 256KB.

**Pagination**: list endpoints use opaque cursor pagination. Pass `meta.cursor` from the previous response as `?cursor=...` to fetch the next page; do not parse or construct cursors client-side.

tags.name: Markets
tags.description: Cached Kalshi and Polymarket market data.

tags.name: User
tags.description: User profile, wallet, and devnet helpers.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Manage programmatic access keys for the authenticated user.

get.description: List active markets grouped by event across supported venues (Kalshi and Polymarket). Filters: `category`, `venue`, `subcategory` (validated against the selected category), `frequency` (`daily`/`weekly`/`monthly`/`hourly`), `date_filter` (time bucket), and `search`. Pagination is cursor-based at the event level. The response includes `available_subcategories` and `available_frequencies` so clients can drive filter chips from the same result set.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by category. Pass `all` to disable the default category filter.

parameters.name: venue
parameters.description: Filter by venue. Omit to include all venues.

parameters.name: subcategory
parameters.description: Filter events by subcategory. Valid values depend on the selected `category` (requires `category` to also be set).

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.description: Filter events by the cadence of the underlying series. `other` is intentionally rejected — it's a residual response bucket, not a filter target.

parameters.name: date_filter
parameters.description: Time bucket filter. If omitted, a default 7-day horizon is applied. Pass `all` to retrieve all active markets regardless of close date.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Full-text search across event/market titles.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Events per page. Default 21, max 100. The product `limit × markets_per_event` must not exceed 2000.

parameters.name: markets_per_event
parameters.description: Max markets returned per event group. Default 10, max 50.

200.description: Events grouped by event_ticker, plus subcategory/frequency facets for the current result set

get.summary: List markets as a flat feed (programmatic)
get.description:
A flat, cursor-paginated list of individual markets — the programmatic counterpart to `GET /markets`. Unlike that endpoint this does NOT group by event and imposes NO per-event market cap, so it surfaces every market of a busy game (a World Cup fixture carries 70+ markets across prop series — corners, assists, team totals — that the event-grouped listing truncates away). Intended for market makers and partners that need the full tradeable universe.

Every filter is applied server-side against the live market index and results are ordered by 24h volume, descending. Pagination is cursor- based: follow `meta.cursor` until it is null. `meta.total` is the full match count. The feed reflects the search index directly (refreshed continuously), so it is a few seconds fresher than the display listing.

**Completeness is best-effort, not gap-free.** Ordering is by a single key (24h volume) and the long tail this feed targets — props like assists and corners — clusters at volume 0, one large tie group whose internal order is not stable across index mutations (markets re-sync every few minutes; de-whitelisted markets are evicted). Paging offset by offset over several seconds can therefore skip or repeat a market inside that tie group. **Consumers doing a full sync should de-duplicate by `ticker`** and, ideally, re-run periodically rather than treating one pass as authoritative. Narrow filters (a `series_ticker` set or `subcategory`) shrink the result to a stable, fully-enumerable page or two and sidestep this entirely — the recommended pattern for MMs.

To retrieve every market of a single game/event including its long-tail props, prefer `GET /markets/events/{event_ticker}` — it returns one event's complete market set in a single call.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by top-level category. Omit for all categories.

parameters.name: venue
parameters.description: Filter by venue (kalshi or polymarket). Omit to include all venues.

parameters.name: subcategory
parameters.description: Filter by subcategory (e.g. `world_cup`, `mlb`, `btc`). Valid values depend on the selected `category`; requires `category` to also be set.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.description: Filter by the cadence of the underlying series. `other` is not a valid filter input.

parameters.name: series_ticker
parameters.description: Restrict to one or more series. Comma-separated for an OR match (e.g. `KXWCAST,KXWCTCORNERS`). Case-sensitive; at most 100 values.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by market status. Omit for tradeable markets only (default `active,open`). Pass a comma-separated list of `active`, `open`, `closed`, `settled`, `finalized` to override, or `all` to include every status regardless of tradeability. An empty value or an unrecognized status returns 400 (an empty value is rejected rather than silently widening to all statuses).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Markets per page. Default 100, max 500. Pagination is bounded to the first 10,000 matches; beyond that, narrow the filters.

total.description: Total markets matching the filters across all pages.

get.description: Fetch a single Kalshi market by ticker or a single Polymarket market by condition ID. When `venue` is omitted, the API infers Polymarket for condition IDs that look like `0x...`; otherwise it looks up the market as Kalshi.

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: Kalshi market ticker or Polymarket `condition_id`.

parameters.name: venue
parameters.description: Optional venue hint. Use this when the caller already knows whether the ticker belongs to Kalshi or Polymarket.

get.description: Get the authenticated user's profile. Auto-creates a user record on first call. Pass `?ref=<invite-code>` on first login to auto-redeem an invite code; when redemption succeeds the response includes `auto_redeemed: true` and, where configured, an `airdrop` outcome.

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: Referral / invite code to auto-redeem on first login.

put.description: Change the authenticated user's display name. Must be 3–20 characters, alphanumeric and underscores only.

get.description: Check whether a username is available. Must match 3–20 chars, alphanumeric + underscores.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Candidate username.

post.description: Create a new API key for the authenticated user. The full secret is returned **once** in `data.key` — save it immediately. Subsequent listings only show the prefix. Requires Privy JWT auth (web dashboard); cannot be called with an existing API key.

name.description: Optional display name for the key.

201.description: API key created (full key visible once)

get.description: List the authenticated user's API keys. Only metadata and prefixes are returned — the original secret is never re-exposed.

delete.description: Revoke one of the authenticated user's API keys by prefix. Takes effect immediately on all subsequent requests. Returns `204 No Content`.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: Key prefix returned by `listApiKeys` / `createApiKey` (`key_prefix`).

get.description: Get the authenticated user's embedded Solana wallet address, wallet/vault balances, and the aggregated `locked_amount` across active RFQs and vault positions.
Latest Solana blockhash for client-side transaction construction. Useful for browser clients that do not have direct RPC access.

post.description: Submit a client-signed serialized Solana transaction. Used for wallet delegation and other user-signed flows from browser clients without direct RPC access. Rate-limited to 10 requests/minute.

get.summary: Get user vault state and active positions
get.description:
Returns the user's vault balance and active positions. Returns data: null
(200 OK) if the user has no vault yet (vault is created on first trade).

200.description: OK — vault state or null if no vault exists

get.description:
Returns the user's full portfolio in a single batched query: vault balance,
trading stats, RFQ status counts, and active-bet summary. Balance is null
when the user has no vault (never traded).

post.summary: Confirm TEE wallet delegation AND drain any pending welcome airdrop
post.description:
Two responsibilities, kept in one round-trip so the frontend can
render a single combined success toast:

1. **Delegation status.** Confirms whether the embedded wallet
   is currently delegated to our key quorum. The actual
   delegation is a USER-SIDE action (frontend `addSigners()`);
   this endpoint only reads the current state — it does NOT
   perform the delegation itself.
2. **Pending welcome-airdrop drain.** Under the deferred-airdrop
   flow (TT-airdrop-bug 2026-05-13), the welcome airdrop amount
   is staged on `users.welcome_airdrop_pending_amount` at
   approval time. This endpoint atomically claims that amount,
   mints the USDC from treasury to the canonical wallet, and
   clears the column. Idempotent: a second call after a
   successful drain returns `airdrop: null` (nothing left to
   drain) without re-minting. On mint failure, the pending
   amount is restored so the user can retry on their next call.

airdrop.description:
Outcome of the pending-airdrop drain.
`null` when nothing was staged for this
user (most common — pre-approval, or
already drained on a prior call). See
`AirdropDrainOutcome` for the non-null
shape.

post.summary: Withdraw USDC from the embedded wallet to an external address
post.description:
Sends USDC out of the caller's embedded wallet ATA to an arbitrary
on-curve Solana address. Uses Privy's session-signer delegation
(set up via /v1/wallet/enable-trading) and Privy's native gas
sponsorship — the user is not prompted to sign and never needs SOL.

Chained vault-tap: when the requested `amount_usdc` exceeds the
caller's ATA-available balance, the handler first issues
`withdraw_from_vault` (vault PDA → owner ATA) for the shortfall,
waits for that TX to confirm, then issues the external SPL transfer.
The `vault_signature` / `vault_amount` fields surface the intermediate
TX when this happens. If the vault tap succeeds but the external
transfer fails to construct or broadcast, the response is **502**
with `error.code: PARTIAL_VAULT_ONLY` and the vault TX surfaced under
`error.details.vault_signature` / `error.details.vault_amount`. The
caller can retry with the same amount; the pre-flight will see the
ATA is now sufficient and skip the vault tap. 502 (rather than 200)
is deliberate: the idempotency middleware caches only 2xx, so
same-key retries against a 502 re-enter the handler instead of
replaying a cached partial response.

Idempotency-Key is honored: a duplicate request with the same key
replays the cached 2xx response without re-issuing on-chain TXs.

recipient.description:
Destination Solana wallet address (base58). Must be
on-curve — PDAs, program IDs, and token-account
addresses are rejected because funds sent to a
derived ATA-of-an-ATA are unrecoverable.

amount_usdc.description:
USDC amount to send. Minimum is one USDC base unit
(1e-6). Must be ≤ the caller's total spendable pool
(ATA + vault free_balance − in-flight RFQ holds);
requests exceeding this are rejected with 400.

200.description:
Withdrawal submitted. `signature` is the external SPL transfer
signature. `vault_signature` and `vault_amount` are present
only when the request required a vault → ATA hop.

explorer_url.description: Solscan URL for the external SPL signature.

vault_signature.description:
On-chain `withdraw_from_vault` signature.
Present only when the request required a vault
→ ATA hop to cover a shortfall.

vault_amount.description:
USDC amount moved from vault to ATA before the
external transfer. Present only when
`vault_signature` is present.

recipient_ata_created.description:
Present (true) when the recipient had no USDC token
account and this transaction created one (the
create-and-chargeback path, gated by
WITHDRAW_ATA_AUTOCREATE). The fee-payer fronts the
~0.002 SOL account rent and bills it back in USDC, so
the recipient receives `delivered_usdc` and the treasury
keeps `chargeback_usdc`. The create is non-idempotent, so
a confirmed response means this tx created the account
(a race where it already existed reverts and retries
via the standard path, with no chargeback).

chargeback_usdc.description:
USDC withheld from the requested amount to reimburse
the one-time recipient-ATA rent. Present only with
`recipient_ata_created`.

delivered_usdc.description:
USDC actually delivered to the recipient
(`amount_usdc − chargeback_usdc`). Present only with
`recipient_ata_created`.

409.description:
The caller's embedded wallet is no longer delegated for
server-side signing. Re-enabling trading from the app restores
the session signer.

502.description:
Ambiguous partial state — returned as 5xx (not 200) so
Idempotency-Key callers retry against the handler instead of
replaying a cached partial response. Two codes:

`PARTIAL_VAULT_ONLY` — the vault tap succeeded but the external SPL
transfer failed to construct/broadcast. Funds moved from vault to
ATA; retry the same request to complete the external leg (the
pre-flight sees the updated ATA and skips the vault tap). Returned
only when the vault tap fired this request; a same-key retry against
an already-topped-up ATA skips the tap (`shortfall = 0`) and a
subsequent SPL failure surfaces as a normal 500.

`WITHDRAW_SUBMITTED_UNCONFIRMED` — only on the recipient-ATA
create-and-chargeback path (WITHDRAW_ATA_AUTOCREATE), where broadcast
and confirmation are separate steps: the transaction was broadcast
(`details.signature` present) but confirmation timed out, so it may
have landed. Do NOT blind-retry — verify the recipient balance first,
since a retry would re-send via the now-existing ATA and double-pay.

signature.description:
The broadcast transaction signature. Present on
WITHDRAW_SUBMITTED_UNCONFIRMED — the tx may have
landed; check it before retrying.

chargeback_usdc.description:
On WITHDRAW_SUBMITTED_UNCONFIRMED, the USDC that
would have been withheld for the recipient-ATA rent.

delivered_usdc.description:
On WITHDRAW_SUBMITTED_UNCONFIRMED, the USDC that
would have been delivered to the recipient.

get.description: List your parlays (RFQs), newest first, with cursor pagination. Filter by status (`?status=settled`, or comma-separated `?status=open,quoted`); `?include=quotes` embeds each parlay's quotes.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by RFQ status. Repeatable values are joined with commas (e.g. ?status=open,quoted,accepted).

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Include quotes array (otherwise null)

get.description: Fetch one of your parlays (RFQs) by id, with its quotes. Once the parlay is terminal the response also carries its settlement detail — per-leg outcomes, final status, payout, and the settle/buyback transaction signature. An id you don't own returns 404.
Realized P&L per day over the requested window (`1D` / `1W` / `1M` / `ALL`; default `1W`). Stats totals (wins / losses / realized P&L) are on `GET /v1/portfolio`.
Returns the current webhook endpoint config (or nulls if never configured) plus the subscribable event catalog. The signing secret is never returned — only whether one is set.

parameters.name: owner_kind
parameters.description: Which endpoint to address: `user` (default) or `mm` (market-maker).

put.description: Replace the endpoint URL and subscribed events. Preserves the signing secret. A fresh endpoint is undeliverable until a secret is set (see rotate-secret).

parameters.name: owner_kind
parameters.description: Which endpoint to address: `user` (default) or `mm` (market-maker).

post.description: Mint a fresh HMAC signing secret, returned ONCE (stored encrypted, never retrievable again). Requires an existing endpoint (set a URL first). Deliveries signed with the old secret stop verifying immediately.

parameters.name: owner_kind
parameters.description: Which endpoint to address: `user` (default) or `mm` (market-maker).

signing_secret.description: The new secret (`whsec_…`). Shown only once.

get.description: Recent delivery attempts (status/attempt metadata, newest first). Empty if no endpoint is configured.

parameters.name: owner_kind
parameters.description: Which endpoint to address: `user` (default) or `mm` (market-maker).

post.description: Re-queue a settled (delivered or dead_letter) delivery, scoped to your own endpoint. Replays reuse the same X-Totalis-Event-Id.

parameters.name: owner_kind
parameters.description: Which endpoint to address: `user` (default) or `mm` (market-maker).

PrivyJWT.description: Privy JWT issued to the web dashboard. Sent as `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`. The Privy session signer underpins all wallet-signed actions.

ApiKey.name: X-API-Key
ApiKey.description: Programmatic API key. Sent as `X-API-Key: <key>`. Generate one from the Totalis dashboard. The same header is accepted on the WebSocket auth message.

CursorParam.name: cursor
CursorParam.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response's `meta.cursor`. Do not construct manually.

MmLimitParam.name: limit
MmLimitParam.description: Page size (MM endpoints default to 50, max 100).

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Client-generated UUID. Successful (2xx) responses are cached for 24h and replayed on retry with the same key. The request body is not part of the cache key — use a fresh UUID whenever the payload differs (e.g. updated odds), otherwise the original response is replayed. Non-2xx responses are not cached.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated caller is not allowed to perform this action

Conflict.description: Resource conflict (duplicate, already taken, invalid status transition, etc.)

retry_after.description: Seconds until retry; set on 429 responses.

limit.description: Maximum number of items requested for this page.

offset.description: Number of items skipped before this page.

total.description: Total number of items available across all pages, when returned by the endpoint.

WinnerSide.description: Winner of a settled parlay position.

PositionStatus.description: Vault position lifecycle. `pending`/`processing` are intermediate states before on-chain creation; `settling`/`cancelling` are intermediate states for settlement/cancellation; `settled_win`/`settled_loss`/`cancelled` are terminal; the `*_mm_release` and `expired` states cover the permissionless-expiry path; the buyback path is `active → bought_back_pending_db → bought_back` (with `reconciling_bought_back_db` as the short-lived reconciler sentinel); `error` means retries are exhausted and manual intervention is required.

SubcategorySlug.description: Subcategory wire format (lowercase snake_case). Valid values depend on the selected `category` and are enforced server-side.

ticker.description: Unique market identifier on its venue. **Kalshi**: market ticker (e.g. `KXBTC-25FEB07-T100000`). **Polymarket**: `condition_id`, the 0x-prefixed hex string from Polymarket's CTF (e.g. `0x4d2…`); not the gamma id, question id, or market slug. Yes/no CLOB token ids are not returned by this API.

event_ticker.description: Event identifier the market belongs to. **Kalshi**: event ticker (e.g. `KXBTC-25FEB07`). **Polymarket**: Gamma `event_slug` (falls back to `market_slug` when no parent event exists).

series_ticker.description: Series identifier the event belongs to. **Kalshi**: series ticker (e.g. `KXBTC`). **Polymarket**: Gamma `market_slug` — Polymarket has no native series concept, so we key whitelisting on the market slug.

bet_group.description: Polymarket-only: section label for the row (e.g. "Match O/U 21.5"). Always null on Kalshi rows.

frequency.description: Cadence of the underlying series: daily/weekly/monthly/hourly/other. Null for categories without timeframe pills.

price_to_beat.description: Reference price for Polymarket up/down markets. Null on Kalshi and on non-up/down Polymarket markets.

venue_url.description: Canonical market URL on its source venue.

exclusion_keys.description: Parlay-correlation keys. Two markets **cannot co-exist in the same parlay if (and only if) their `exclusion_keys` arrays intersect** — i.e. share at least one string. Apply a pure set-intersection; there is no rule table to maintain client-side. The keys encode every correlation rule (cross-series exclusion groups, threshold ladders, same-game sports family/kind/participant correlation, Polymarket sub-events). Keys are opaque and namespaced by reason and scope (e.g. `kp:<gameKey>:moneyline|spread`, `grp:<idx>:<eventKey>`, `fam:<gameKey>:<series>`), and are derived solely from the market itself, so a selected leg can snapshot its keys and compare them later. The plain one-leg-per-event rule (same `event_ticker`) is intentionally not encoded here. Always present; an empty array means the market participates in no correlation rule.

event_ticker.description: Event identifier. **Kalshi**: event ticker (e.g. `KXBTC-25FEB07`). **Polymarket**: Gamma `event_slug` (falls back to `market_slug` when no parent event exists).

tickers.description: Identifiers belonging to this exclusion group. When `scope=event`, these are event identifiers (Kalshi event ticker like `KXBTC-25FEB07`, or Polymarket Gamma `event_slug`). When `scope=series`, these are series identifiers (Kalshi series ticker like `KXBTC`, or Polymarket Gamma `market_slug`). Values are compared against `Leg.event_ticker` / `Market.series_ticker` to detect conflicts.

leg_index.description: Zero-based ordering index for the leg within the RFQ

yes_sub_title.description: Snapshot of `Market.yes_sub_title` at RFQ creation (e.g. team name on a sports market). Lets clients render the side-specific subtitle after the underlying market has rolled out of the live `/markets` cache. Null on binary markets that carry no per-side subtitle and on legs created before this field was persisted.

no_sub_title.description: Snapshot of `Market.no_sub_title` at RFQ creation — opposing-side counterpart to `yes_sub_title`. Same null semantics.

venue_url.description: Canonical URL of the leg's market on its source venue (Kalshi or Polymarket). Hydrated from marketd Redis hashes, falling back to the parlay-leg snapshot when the market hash has been purged.

image_url.description: Image URL for the leg's market. Hydrated from the marketd Redis hash and whitelist metadata, falling back to the parlay-leg snapshot when the market hash has been purged.

expected_expiration_time.description: Expected market resolution time for the leg. Hydrated from marketd Redis hashes, falling back to the parlay-leg snapshot when the market hash has been purged.

current_yes_price.description: Yes ask price at RFQ creation time, decimal probability (0-1)

current_no_price.description: No ask price at RFQ creation time, decimal probability (0-1)

quotes.description: null = not requested (fetched without include=quotes). [] = requested but no quotes yet.

position_pda.description: On-chain position address. Set once status >= confirmed.

position_id.description: On-chain position id — the 16-byte vault_positions.position_id, hex-encoded (32 chars). Distinct from position_pda (a base58 address): this is the id the early-cashout endpoints key on (/internal/positions/cashout-eligibility, POST /v1/cashout-requests). Set once status >= confirmed.

position_error.description: Last error recorded on the vault position. Populated when position_status === 'error'; null otherwise.

is_failed.description: True when the vault position errored (on-chain execution failed). Derived from position_status === 'error'. Omitted (not false) for non-failed parlays.

cashout.description: Set on bought_back (early-cashout) RFQs only — realized buyback P&L.

create_position_tx.description: create_position transaction signature. Set once the on-chain vault position is created (status >= confirmed).

settle_tx.description: settle_position transaction signature — the on-chain proof a settled RFQ paid out. Set on settled RFQs (the settle analogue of buyback_tx).

cancel_tx.description: cancel_position transaction signature. Set on cancelled RFQs.

buyback_tx.description: Buyback (early-cashout) transaction signature, surfaced at the RFQ root by the serializer. Set on bought_back RFQs — the on-chain proof of the cashout, the buyback analogue of settle_tx.

Cashout.description:
Early-cashout (buyback) summary attached to a bought_back RFQ — the
buyback analogue of Settlement. realized_pnl is the user's net P&L
(stake nets out; the directional `amount` is the realized delta, less
the profit fee on the profiting side and the create-time taker fee).

realized_pnl.description: User's realized P&L from the early cashout (USDC, signed).

mm_pays_user.description: True when the MM paid the user (user was up on the position).

cashed_out_at.description: Time the position was bought back / cashed out (the buyback fee insert = finalize moment). The buyback analogue of settled_at; clients sort the History cashed-out row by this. Absent on legacy rows without a fee.

MmRfqLeg.description: Subset of a leg surfaced to market makers — drops display-only fields, keeps the price snapshot needed for quoting.

leg_index.description: Zero-based leg position within the RFQ.

market_ticker.description: Unique market identifier on the leg's venue. **Kalshi**: market ticker (e.g. `KXBTC-25FEB07-T100000`). **Polymarket**: `condition_id`, the 0x-prefixed hex string from Polymarket's CTF (e.g. `0x4d2…`); not the gamma id, question id, or market slug. Yes/no CLOB token ids are not returned — resolve them client-side via Polymarket's CLOB if needed.

event_ticker.description: Event identifier the leg's market belongs to. **Kalshi**: event ticker (e.g. `KXBTC-25FEB07`). **Polymarket**: Gamma `event_slug` (falls back to `market_slug` when no parent event exists).

current_yes_price.description: Yes price at RFQ creation, decimal (0-1).

current_no_price.description: No price at RFQ creation, decimal (0-1).

MmRfq.description: Open RFQ visible to market makers.

user_id.description: Privy DID of the bettor (intentionally surfaced to MMs).

status.description: MMs only see RFQs available for quoting.

user_stake.description: Net stake the MM underwrites = bet_amount - taker_fee, where taker_fee = floor(bet_amount_micro * taker_fee_bps / 10000) is computed in integer microUSDC (1 USDC = 1e6 micro). Price and size against this, not bet_amount, when the taker fee is on.

payout_odds.description: Multiplier on the net stake (`user_stake`) that produces the total payout if the parlay wins. When the taker fee is off, `user_stake == bet_amount`.

user_cost.description: Net stake the MM quotes on = bet_amount - taker_fee (== bet_amount when the taker fee is off).

total_payout.description: Total payout on a win = `user_cost + mm_cost`.

leg_prices.description: Per-leg price snapshot captured at submit time. `leg_odds` is a decimal (0-1).

QuoteMmView.description: Quote with MM-only fields surfaced on MM endpoints.

legs.description: Parlay legs (2-5 required). Each leg references a market by ticker on a specific venue.

market_ticker.description: Identifier of the underlying market on its venue. **Kalshi**: market ticker (e.g. `KXBTC-25FEB07-T100000`). **Polymarket**: `condition_id`, the 0x-prefixed hex string from Polymarket's CTF (e.g. `0x4d2…`); not the gamma id, question id, or market slug. Look this up via `GET /markets` (`Market.ticker`) — there is no separate yes/no CLOB token id field; both sides of a Polymarket binary share the same `condition_id` and are distinguished by `side`.

bet_amount.description: USDC. Max 100. Must be a positive finite number.

expires_in_seconds.description: RFQ lifetime in seconds. Floor 30s, cap 24h. Defaults to 300.

reason.description: Optional cancellation reason. Defaults to "User cancelled". Truncated to 500 chars.

payout_odds.description: Multiplier (1.0001x – 1000x). Must be a finite number.

leg_prices.description: Optional per-leg price snapshot. Each entry is `{ leg_id, odds }` where `odds` is a decimal (0-1). Note: the response shape (`Quote.leg_prices[]`) uses `leg_odds` for the same value — the request field is named `odds`, the response field is named `leg_odds`.

confirmation_deadline.description:
Deadline by which the market maker must call `POST /mm/quotes/{quoteId}/confirm`. If the MM misses this deadline, the RFQ expiry sweep transitions both rows based on the RFQ's own `expires_at`:

- **RFQ still within `expires_at`** — RFQ reverts to `open` (with `accepted_quote_id` and `market_maker_id` cleared so other MMs can quote again) and the accepted quote becomes `withdrawn`.
- **RFQ past `expires_at`** — RFQ becomes `expired` and the accepted quote becomes `expired`.

The accepted quote never transitions to `rejected` via the deadline path. `rejected` is reserved for sibling pending quotes auto-rejected when the user accepts a different quote, and for explicit user rejection via `POST /rfqs/{id}/quotes/{quoteId}/reject`.

execution_id.description: Identifier for the queued vault-position-creation job.

id.description: Privy DID — internal identifier, not exposed on public endpoints.

username.description: Display name. Auto-set to the wallet address on first login; can be updated via `PUT /v1/username` to a 3-20 char alphanumeric.

approved.description: True once the user has redeemed a valid invite code or has been admin-approved.

waitlisted.description: True when `approved=false` and the user is on the waitlist; false otherwise. Always present.

auto_redeemed.description: Only present when the `?ref` param successfully redeemed an invite code in this exact request.

airdrop.description: Welcome-airdrop outcome from the auto-redeem path. Present alongside `auto_redeemed: true`. `null` when the invite has no airdrop configured or the user was already credited.

AirdropOutcome.description: Welcome airdrop outcome. `success: true` with `pending: true` means the amount was staged and will mint on the user's next `POST /v1/wallet/enable-trading` call. `success: false` means staging failed.

address.description: Solana wallet address (base58). Null if no wallet is linked yet.

vault_balance.description: Gross USDC balance currently held in the caller's Totalis vault. Resolved by the returned `address`; returns 0 when no vault row exists for that wallet. Includes both free vault cash and locked collateral. Add to `usdc_balance` for total on-chain USDC across wallet and vault; subtract `locked_amount` from that total for an available-cash view.

locked_amount.description: Every USDC the caller has committed across roles: in-flight RFQs, bettor-side vault position stakes, and maker-side `locked_collateral`. Because `vault_balance` is gross, vault locked collateral is intentionally represented in both `vault_balance` and `locked_amount`; compute available cash as `usdc_balance + vault_balance - locked_amount` and do not add `/v1/vault.locked_collateral` again.

has_traded.description: True once any of the caller's parlays has reached `executed` or `settled`. Informational account state; withdrawals are allowed regardless of this value.

ApiKeyFull.description: Returned only on creation; the full `key` is visible exactly once.

payout.description:
Full pot transferred to the winner — equals user_stake + mm_risk
(i.e. bet_amount * payout_odds). NOT just the MM-side movement.
For MM PnL on a loss, use -(payout - user_stake) = -mm_risk.

user_stake.description:
Bettor's stake at settle time. Surfaced so the MM dashboard can
derive PnL without a second fetch — MM win = +user_stake - fee,
MM loss = -(payout - user_stake) = -mm_risk.

mm_risk.description:
Maker's max risk at settle time. Equal to payout - user_stake; the
MM dashboard renders loss-side PnL directly off this field.

fee_amount.description: Protocol fee deducted from the winner's payout.

AirdropDrainOutcome.description:
Outcome of the pending-airdrop drain performed by
`POST /v1/wallet/enable-trading`. Narrower than
`AirdropOutcome` — there is no `pending` field because the drain
is the terminal step (either the mint landed or it didn't; the
amount is never left in a staged state by this endpoint).
`{ success: true, amount }` — the staged amount minted
successfully on-chain. `{ success: false, amount }` — the mint
attempt failed and the pending amount has been restored; the
user can retry on their next call.

ready.description: True if a wallet is linked and delegation can proceed

delegated.description: True if TEE wallet delegation is active (server can sign)

address.description: Solana wallet address (base58). Empty string if no wallet linked.

wallet_id.description: Privy embedded wallet ID (used for delegation API calls)

PortfolioBalance.description:
Caller's vault balance (DB-cached, no RPC). Null only when the
caller has no vault row at all. For dual-role accounts the
bettor-side vault is preferred and the maker-side row is used
as a fallback (same rules as `GET /v1/vault`).

balance.description: Null when user has no vault (never traded)

PortfolioSummary.description: Derived portfolio-level aggregates from active positions.

active_bet_total.description: Sum of bet_amount across active RFQs

portfolio_value.description: Gross vault balance (deposited USDC). Does not include unrealized P&L.

executed.description: Positions on-chain but not yet resolved. Distinct from total_settled — settled means market resolution + payout distributed.

total_failed.description: Parlays where the vault position errored on-chain. Derived from vault_positions.status = 'error'.

updated_at.description: Null when the user has never settled a trade (no rfq.user_stats row yet).

UserVaultResponse.description:
Vault state with active position summaries (GET /v1/vault response).
Returns the caller's bettor-side vault (owner_type='user') when one
exists; falls back to their maker-side vault (owner_type='mm') for
pure-MM accounts so the portfolio surface reflects every USDC the
caller controls. `positions` is the bettor-side active list when
returning the user vault, or the maker-side active list (joined via
`q.market_maker_id`) when returning the MM vault.

VaultPositionSummary.description:
Lightweight position summary for the vault response.

Caller-relative reading: `user_stake` and `mm_risk` are
side-of-trade labels, not caller-relative ones. When
`UserVaultResponse` returns the bettor-side vault, `user_stake`
is the caller's own stake. When it returns the maker-side
vault (pure-MM accounts), `user_stake` is the counterparty
bettor's stake and `mm_risk` is the caller's own collateral.
Don't display `user_stake` as "my stake" without branching on
which vault was returned — for aggregate "USDC committed",
prefer `wallet.locked_amount`, which is role-agnostic.

user_stake.description:
Bettor side of the trade. The caller's stake on the
bettor-side vault response; the counterparty's stake on
the maker-side vault response.

mm_risk.description:
Maker side of the trade. The counterparty's collateral on
the bettor-side vault response; the caller's own
collateral on the maker-side vault response.

url.description: Configured HTTPS endpoint, or null if never set.

status.description: Endpoint status, or null if never configured.

has_signing_secret.description: Whether a signing secret is set (deliveries require one).

event_catalog.description: The events this endpoint kind may subscribe to. (GET only.)

url.description: HTTPS endpoint. Must not resolve to private/internal infrastructure.

events.description: Event names to subscribe to (subset of the catalog). Empty parks the endpoint.
